email.description: Email is the email address for Let's Encrypt registration

server.description:
Server is the ACME server URL (defaults to Let's Encrypt production)
Use "https://acme-staging-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory" for testing

config.AzureBackupTarget.description: AzureBackupTarget configures a Longhorn-native azblob:// backup target.

config.BackupSchedules.description: BackupSchedules holds the two RecurringJob schedules.

config.CACertRef.description: CACertRef references a pre-existing Secret or ConfigMap key holding a PEM CA bundle.

config.CertEnv.description: CertEnv names environment variables holding raw (non-base64) PEM material.

config.CertFiles.description: CertFiles points at PEM cert/key files on disk.

type.description: Type is the certificate type: "selfsigned", "letsencrypt", or "existing"

secret_name.description:
SecretName overrides the name of the TLS secret the gateway references.
Defaults to "nebari-gateway-tls". For type=existing with existing_secret,
the gateway references ExistingSecret.Name instead.

existing_secret.description:
ExistingSecret references a kubernetes.io/tls secret the user already created.
Mutually exclusive with Files and Env. Only valid when Type=existing.

files.description:
Files reads PEM material from disk; NIC creates the secret directly.
Mutually exclusive with ExistingSecret and Env. Only valid when Type=existing.

env.description:
Env reads raw PEM material from environment variables; NIC creates the secret directly.
Mutually exclusive with ExistingSecret and Files. Only valid when Type=existing.

config.ClusterConfig.description: ClusterConfig holds typed cloud provider configuration.

config.DNSConfig.description: DNSConfig holds typed DNS provider configuration.

namespace.description: Namespace is the secret's namespace. Defaults to envoy-gateway-system.

config.ExistingSecretRef.description: ExistingSecretRef references a pre-existing TLS secret.

allow_recurring_job_while_volume_detache.description:
AllowRecurringJobWhileVolumeDetached maps to the cluster-wide Longhorn
Setting. nil defaults to true (the pack's behaviour): JupyterHub user PVCs
detach when servers idle out, and Longhorn's stock default of false would
silently skip them at the cron tick.

config.LonghornBackupConfig.description: LonghornBackupConfig drives the Longhorn snapshot/backup schedules, the cluster Setting, and the S3/azblob BackupTarget + credential Secret.

cluster.description:
Cluster Provider configuration.
Only one provider can be configured at a time.

dns.description:
DNS provider configuration (optional).
Only one provider can be configured at a time.

git_repository.description: GitRepository configures the GitOps repository for ArgoCD bootstrap (optional)

trust_bundle.description:
TrustBundle, when set, propagates an enterprise CA bundle both to worker-node
OS trust stores (via the cluster provider) and into the cluster via
trust-manager. Required when egress is TLS-inspected by a corporate proxy.

backups.description: Backups configures off-cluster backup scheduling (Longhorn). Optional.

config.NebariConfig.description: NebariConfig represents the parsed nebari-config.yaml structure

config.S3BackupTarget.description: S3BackupTarget configures an AWS-native or S3-compatible backup target.

config.TrustBundleConfig.description: TrustBundleConfig specifies the source of an extra CA bundle.

ssh_key_env.description:
SSHKeyEnv is the name of the environment variable containing the SSH private key
The key should be in PEM format (e.g., contents of ~/.ssh/id_ed25519)

token_env.description:
TokenEnv is the name of the environment variable containing the personal access token
Used for HTTPS authentication

insecure_skip_host_key_verification.description:
InsecureSkipHostKeyVerification disables SSH host key verification,
removing protection against man-in-the-middle attacks. Only intended
for ephemeral environments (e.g. CI) where maintaining a known_hosts
file is impractical. Has no effect on token (HTTPS) authentication.

git.AuthConfig.description: AuthConfig specifies authentication credentials for git operations.

url.description:
URL is the repository URL (SSH or HTTPS format)
Examples: "git@github.com:org/repo.git" or "https://github.com/org/repo.git"

branch.description: Branch is the git branch to use (default: "main")

path.description:
Path is an optional subdirectory within the repository
If specified, all operations are scoped to this path

auth.description: Auth specifies credentials for NIC to push to the repository (requires write access)

argocd_auth.description:
ArgoCDAuth specifies optional separate credentials for ArgoCD (read-only access)
If not specified, falls back to Auth

git.Config.description: Config represents git repository configuration for GitOps bootstrap.
